mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in nonfarm payrolls signals weakening aggregate income and consumption demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between PAYEMS growth and 20-day returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0% reflects restrictive monetary policy that raises real discount rates and corporate borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 50% of months when the trigger is active over a 10-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising unemployment and a federal funds rate above 3.0% signals a policy-mistake recession, where tight monetary policy coincides with labor market deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2pp over 3 months while FEDFUNDS > 3.0%
failure_condition: The joint signal generates positive average 60-day returns in more than 40% of triggered months, or standalone UNRATE and FEDFUNDS rank correlations exceed the interaction model.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month contraction in industrial production predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days during late-cycle expansions when capacity constraints and margin compression amplify the signal.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO momentum fails to predict negative returns in late-cycle regimes, with mean forward return t-statistic greater than zero over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1100 thousand units signal a contraction in residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and consumer durables, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 55% of months when HOUST is below 1100 over a 10-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation accelerating above 3.0% year-over-year erodes real corporate earnings and pressures the Federal Reserve to maintain tight monetary policy, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are not significantly negative during high-inflation periods, with a one-sided t-test p-value exceeding 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in real GDP signals durable aggregate demand and revenue growth for listed corporations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns remains below 0.02 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration that reduces household income and consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 50% of trigger instances during walk-forward evaluation from 1990 to 2010.
